perturbation.original_text: (g) Confidential Information. The term "Confidential Information' means any and all data and information relating to the business conducted by the Company (whether constituting a Trade Secret or not) which is or has been disclosed to Master Distributor or of which Master Distributor became aware as a consequence of or through his relationship with Company and which has value to the Company and is not generally known by its competitors; provided, however, no information will be deemed "confidential" unless such information is treated by the Company as confidential. Confidential Information shall not include any data or information that has been voluntarily disclosed to the public by the Company (except where such public disclosure has been made by Master Distributor or any other person or entity without authorization), or that has been independently developed and disclosed by others (except where such independent development and disclosure has been made by Master Distributor or any other person or entity without authorization), or that otherwise enters the public domain through lawful means.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) Confidential Information. The term "Confidential Information' means any and all data and information relating to the business conducted by the Company (whether constituting a Trade Secret or not) which is or has been disclosed to Master Distributor or of which Master Distributor became aware as a consequence of or through his relationship with Company and which has value to the Company and is not generally known by its competitors; provided, however, all information will be deemed “confidential” regardless if such information is treated by the Company as confidential. Confidential Information shall not include any data or information that has been voluntarily disclosed to the public by the Company (except where such public disclosure has been made by Master Distributor or any other person or entity without authorization), or that has been independently developed and disclosed by others (except where such independent development and disclosure has been made by Master Distributor or any other person or entity without authorization), or that otherwise enters the public domain through lawful means.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of "Confidential Information." Initially, information is only deemed confidential if the Company treats it as such. The change removes this condition, stating that all information related to the business is confidential regardless of the Company's treatment of it. This contradicts Section 5, which restricts non-disclosure of Confidential Information for three years following termination, because now all information would be confidential which creates uncertainty about what specific information the Master Distributor is restricted from disclosing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Master Distributor shall not, during the term of this Agreement and the Master Distributorship Agreement, and for a period of three (3) years following termination, for any reason, of the Master Distributorship Agreement, disclose, use, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer, or make available, directly or indirectly, to any person, business concern, or other entity, any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information except in the proper performance of its duties hereunder; provided, however, Master Distributor is not prohibited hereby from disclosing or using any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information which subsequently becomes part of the public domain through no breach of this Agreement and the Master Distributorship Agreement and through no fault of Master Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The original text states information is only considered confidential if the company treats it as such. The changed text removes this condition, stating that ALL information related to the business is confidential, regardless of whether the company treats it as such. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which restricts the Master Distributor from disclosing 'Confidential Information' for three years post-termination. By broadening the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include all business-related information, it creates uncertainty and contradiction regarding what specific information the Master Distributor is prohibited from disclosing as, previously, it was only information the company considered confidential.

perturbation.original_text: (p) Territory. The term "Territory" means the world.
perturbation.changed_text: (p) Territory. The term "Territory" means the United States.
perturbation.explanation: The change contradicts the initial definition of "Territory" as 'the world' by restricting it to only 'the United States'. This contradicts section 16, which states that litigation will take place in Charleston County, South Carolina, implying the agreement is enforceable outside the US. Now that the territory is only the US, this agreement would have no force outside the US.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be interpreted and governed by the laws of the State of South Carolina, without giving effect to its conflicts of laws rules. Notwithstanding alternative dispute settlement provisions in the Master Distributorship Agreement, any action or proceeding to enforce or interpret this Agreement shall be brought only in the state or federal courts sitting in Charleston County, South Carolina, United States, and Master Distributor hereby irrevocably submits and consents to such exclusive jurisdiction and venue.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of "Territory" from 'the world' to 'the United States'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that litigation will occur in Charleston County, South Carolina and that the agreement is governed by the laws of South Carolina. The original text allows for the agreement to be interpreted in Charleston County, South Carolina, for the whole world. By changing 'Territory' to only include the United States, the changed text contradicts the original text as it limits the agreements' enforcement to only the US, even though the agreement states that it can be enforced in South Carolina, which implies it has some enforcement or interpretation outside of South Carolina. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, as the different sections of a contract (definition of Territory and jurisdiction clause) provide conflicting interpretations of the scope of the agreement, leading to a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (e) Competing Business. 'Me term "Competing Business" means any person or entity in the same business or substantially the same business as the Business of Company.
perturbation.changed_text: (e) Competing Business. The term "Competing Business" means any person or entity in direct competition with the Company, offering identical products or services within a 50-mile radius of the Company's principal place of business.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Competing Business' is broad, covering any entity in the same or substantially the same business. The modified definition narrows it to only those offering identical products or services within a 50-mile radius. This creates a conflict with section 8 regarding the protection of Intellectual Property. The Company should have the sole right to protect the Intellectual Property from legal action but the definition change suggests that any other person or entity outside of the 50-mile radius of the Company would not be considered a 'Competing Business'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Master Distributor agrees to cooperate fully with Company to protect company's proprietary rights in the Intellectual Property, yet acknowledges and agrees that Company shall have the sole right, opportunity and duty to protect the Intellectual Property from legal action or suit for infringement thereof, and Master Distributor shall not respond, contest, settle, or otherwise conduct any proceedings or handle any claims without prior consultation with and written approval from Company, unless a response is necessary to avoid compromising the rights of Company in such proceedings or with regard to such claims.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Competing Business' was broad, encompassing any entity in the same or substantially the same business. The changed definition narrows it to entities offering identical products/services within a 50-mile radius. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text', which grants the Company the sole right to protect its Intellectual Property from legal action. The narrowed definition implies entities outside the 50-mile radius, even if infringing on IP, might not be considered 'Competing Businesses' under the modified definition, thus weakening the company's ability to protect its IP and directly contradicting the stated duty and rights.

perturbation.original_text: (b) ACT Electronic Reader. The term "ACT Electronic Reader" means the device produced by or on behalf of the Company to read the Cards.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) ACT Electronic Reader. The term "ACT Electronic Reader" means the device produced by the Master Distributor to read the Cards.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition states the 'ACT Electronic Reader' is produced by the Company. The changed definition directly contradicts this by stating it is produced by the Master Distributor. This introduces conflict into Section 7 regarding the license and use of Marks. If the Master Distributor is producing the reader, Company is not supposed to give rights to use the marks associated with the reader.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company hereby grants to Master Distributor, during the term of this Agreement only, the non-exclusive, royalty-free limited right and license to use the Marks only in connection with the performance of Master Distributor's duties and obligations under this Agreement and under the Master Distributorship Agreement. Master Distributor agrees not to use the Marks in connection with any other business, products or services. Master Distributor agrees not to use the Marks, or any of them, or any confusingly similar name or symbol, in whole or in part, as part of Master Distributor's business or trade name. The Parties shall mutually approve all promotional material used by Master Distributor in connection with the distribution and marketing of the Products to ensure that Master Distributor properly uses the Marks.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the 'ACT Electronic Reader' as being produced by the Company, while the changed text states it is produced by the Master Distributor. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants the Master Distributor a license to use the Marks in connection with their duties under the agreement. If the Master Distributor is now producing the reader (which is associated with the marks), it creates a conflict with the granted license, as the company should be granting use of marks for its own product, not one produced by Master Distributor. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: (o) Proprietary Information. The term "Proprietary Information" means all of the following materials and information, whether or not patentable or protected or protectable, by copyright or equivalent design, registration law or regulation existing from time to time with any part of the Territory, to which Master Distributor has received access or which Master Distributor receives, develops, or has developed, in whole or in part, as a direct or indirect result of performing this Agreement or through the use of any of Company's facilities or resources: (i) Production processes, quality control processes and/or procedures, marketing techniques, purchasing information, pricing policies, quoting procedures, financial information, customer names and requirements, customer data and other materials or information relating to the manner in which Company does business; (ii) Discoveries, concepts and ideas, and the embodiment thereof, whether or not patentable or subject to protection by a copyright, I including, without limitation, the nature and results of research and development activities, processes, formulas, techniques and "know-how"; (iii) Any other materials or information related to the business or activities of Company which are not generally known to others engaged in similar business or activities; (iv) Trade Secrets, Confidential Information or other proprietary information which Company has acquired or may in the future acquire from any third party, including, without limitation, operating principles, documentation, drawings, programs and performance specifications and results, provided to Company by such third parties pursuant to agreements, understandings and/or acknowledgments to the effect that such trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information provided to Company by such third parties (collectively "Third Party Confidential Information") is the proprietary and/or confidential information of such respective third party and is to be treated by Company as if such Third Party Confidential Information were Company's Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: (o) Proprietary Information. The term "Proprietary Information" means only the specific documents explicitly marked as "Proprietary" by the Company and provided directly to the Master Distributor. It excludes any information the Master Distributor independently develops or learns, even if related to the Company's business.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of Proprietary Information is broad and includes information developed by the Master Distributor as a result of the agreement or using Company resources. The modified definition severely restricts it to only explicitly marked documents provided by the Company, excluding independently developed information. This creates a conflict with section 3, which states that all Proprietary Information is the exclusive property of the Company, even if the Master Distributor uses or is aware of it. The new definition would suggest information developed by the Master Distributor is not the company's property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Master Distributor agrees and acknowledges that all Proprietary Information, Intellectual Property, Trade Secrets and Confidential Information, and any and all embodiments thereof (herein, the "Property"), is and shall remain at all times the exclusive property of and owned by the Company, and that Master Distributor's performance of its duties and obligations and its use or awareness of the Property during the term of this Agreement or the Master Distributorship Agreement shall create no rights, at law or in equity, under the laws and decisions of any nation or state within the Territory, in Master Distributor in or to the Property, or any aspect or embodiment thereof. Master Distributor further agrees not to copy, reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or otherwise reproduce any Product or its equivalent, in whole or in part.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Proprietary Information' significantly narrows its scope to only explicitly marked documents provided by the Company, excluding information independently developed by the Master Distributor. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that *all* Proprietary Information, including any awareness or use of it by the Master Distributor, remains the exclusive property of the Company. The change creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text implies that information developed by the master distributor that relates to the company is still considered proprietary information, but the changed text implies it isn't unless it's explicitly marked.

perturbation.original_text: (q) Trade Secrets. The term "Trade Secrets" means the whole or any portion or phase of any data or information developed, owned, or licensed from a third party by the Company, including any formula, pattern, compilation, program, device, method, technique, improvement, or process that: (i) derives independent economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to, and not being readily ascertainable through proper means by, other persons who can obtain economic value from its disclosure or use, and (ii) is the subject of efforts that are reasonable under the circumstances to maintain its secrecy. Trade Secrets shall not include any data or information (i) that has been voluntarily disclosed to the public by Company or has become generally known to the public (except when such public disclosure has been made by or through Master Distributor, or by a third person or entity with the knowledge of Master Distributor, without authorization from Company); (ii) that has been independently developed and disclosed by parties other than Master Distributor or Company, the public generally or to Master Distributor without a breach of obligation of confidentiality by any such parties running directly or indirectly to Company; or (iii) that otherwise enters the public domain through lawful means.
perturbation.changed_text: (q) Trade Secrets. The term "Trade Secrets" means only those specific items identified in Exhibit A attached to this agreement and signed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Trade Secrets' is detailed and encompasses various types of data and information that meet specific criteria related to economic value and secrecy. The modified definition drastically simplifies it, limiting Trade Secrets to only those items explicitly listed in Exhibit A. This creates a contradiction with section 4, which concerns the non-disclosure of Trade Secrets. The original definition, Master Distributor shall not disclose Trade Secrets at anytime following termination, but the change states Trade Secrets are limited to those in Exhibit A, which is conflicting in how much information is protected after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Master Distributor shall not, during the term of this Agreement and the Master Distributorship Agreement, and at any and all times following termination, for any reason, of this Agreement or the Master Distributorship Agreement, disclose, use, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer, or make available, directly or indirectly, to any person, business concern, or other entity, any Trade Secrets except in the proper performance of its duties hereunder; provided, however, Master Distributor is not prohibited hereby from disclosing or using any Trade Secrets which subsequently becomes part of the public domain through no breach of this Agreement or the Master Distributorship Agreement and through no fault of Master Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of "Trade Secrets" to only include items listed in Exhibit A. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which broadly prohibits the Master Distributor from disclosing any Trade Secrets at any time, even after termination of the agreement. The original definition was much wider, but the changed definition restricts Trade Secrets to specific items. This creates a contradiction as the Master Distributor is restricted to disclose a wide array of information after termination in the Contradicted Text, but the Change text limits trade secrets to items in Exhibit A.
